_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning that paragraphs group related information together and are an important way to organise our writing.

_source.w: We are learning that paragraphs group related information together and are an important way to organise our writing.
y: Paragraphs help readers follow our thinking by breaking ideas into manageable chunks.
Well-organised paragraphs make our writing clearer and more professional.
Understanding paragraphs is essential for writing longer, more complex texts.
eg: We identify paragraphs in a text and explain what each one is about.
We practise starting a new paragraph when we move to a new idea or topic.
We reorganise a jumbled text by grouping sentences into logical paragraphs.
We write a three-paragraph text with an introduction, body and conclusion.
We compare a well-paragraphed text with one big block of text and discuss which is easier to read.
We use topic sentences to signal what each paragraph is about.
